perturbation.original_text: 4.1 Project Manager. The Manager of the Project (the "Project Manager") --------------- shall be Scientific Measurement Systems, Inc. ("SMS"), a Texas corporation. The Project Manager shall be the single point of contact to NIST, representing the Participants and the Management Committee referred to below. The Project Manager shall perform the day-to-day management and administration of the Project in accordance with this Agreement and the Cooperative Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1 Project Manager. The Manager of the Project (the "Project Manager") --------------- shall be General Electric Company (“GE”), a corporation. The Project Manager shall be the single point of contact to NIST, representing the Participants and the Management Committee referred to below. While the Project Manager is responsible for high-level strategy, each Participant shall manage the day-to-day administration of its own tasks.
perturbation.explanation: Originally, SMS was designated as the Project Manager. By changing this to GE and stating each Participant manages its day-to-day tasks, there is an in-text contradiction that significantly alters the responsibility and authority for project management. This change contradicts any section referring to SMS as the Project Manager or its responsibilities for day-to-day administration and oversight for the entire Project.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1 Project Manager. The Manager of the Project (the "Project Manager") --------------- shall be General Electric Company (“GE”), a corporation. The Project Manager shall be the single point of contact to NIST, representing the Participants and the Management Committee referred to below. While the Project Manager is responsible for high-level strategy, each Participant shall manage the day-to-day administration of its own tasks.
perturbation.justification: The original text designated Scientific Measurement Systems, Inc. (SMS) as the Project Manager with responsibility for day-to-day management. The changed text replaces SMS with General Electric Company (GE) as the Project Manager but then states that each Participant shall manage the day-to-day administration of its own tasks. This creates a direct contradiction because it removes the Project Manager's responsibility for day-to-day administration and assigns it to individual Participants, which directly conflicts with the initial definition of the Project Manager's role and introduces ambiguity regarding overall project management responsibilities. Since the Contradicted text is actually the Change text. and the change creates a strong contradiction based on the explanation, the answer should be YES

perturbation.original_text: 6.2 Ownership. Subject to any rights the United States government may have --------- under the Cooperative Agreement, upon the conclusion or termination of this Agreement the title to, and possession of, all Project hardware or software shall be as follows: (a) Early Termination. In the event that this Agreement is terminated ----------------- prior to successful completion of the Project, the parties agree that each Participant shall have title to, and possession of, that portion of the "bread board" apparatus and/or "demonstrator" apparatus which said Participant contributed as part of its cost share contribution, and SMS shall have title to, and possession of, (a) all other portions of said "bread board" apparatus and/or "demonstrator" apparatus developed prior to termination, as well as (b) any and all acceptable detector panels produced by Project funded production runs. In the event of such an early termination, each Participant shall have title to, and possession of, any purchased software which forms part of its "in kind" contribution to the Project, and SMS shall have title to, and possession of, all other software purchased for the Project.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.2 Ownership. Subject to any rights the United States government may have --------- under the Cooperative Agreement, upon the conclusion or termination of this Agreement the title to, and possession of, all Project hardware or software shall be as follows: (a) Early Termination. In the event that this Agreement is terminated ----------------- prior to successful completion of the Project, all Project hardware and software shall be jointly owned by all Participants in equal shares, regardless of contribution. Any Participant may use such hardware or software for internal research purposes, but commercialization requires unanimous consent from all Participants. In the event of such an early termination, all Participants shall also have shared ownership of all software.
perturbation.explanation: This edit introduces a direct contradiction regarding ownership of Project hardware and software in the event of early termination. The original text details specific allocation of hardware and software to contributing parties, whereas the altered text establishes joint ownership in equal shares for all participants, irrespective of their contribution. This creates an in-text contradiction because the original section allocates ownership based on contribution, while the modified section disregards contribution and mandates joint ownership. This uncertainty could lead to disputes over usage and rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.2 Ownership. Subject to any rights the United States government may have --------- under the Cooperative Agreement, upon the conclusion or termination of this Agreement the title to, and possession of, all Project hardware or software shall be as follows: (a) Early Termination. In the event that this Agreement is terminated ----------------- prior to successful completion of the Project, all Project hardware and software shall be jointly owned by all Participants in equal shares, regardless of contribution. Any Participant may use such hardware or software for internal research purposes, but commercialization requires unanimous consent from all Participants. In the event of such an early termination, all Participants shall also have shared ownership of all software.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 6.2(a) directly contradicts the original intent of Section 6.2 regarding ownership of Project hardware and software upon early termination. The original text specifies allocation based on contribution, while the altered text enforces joint ownership regardless of contribution. This creates a clear and direct conflict, leading to uncertainty and potential disputes, therefore a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: 8.9 Preferred Customers: Practical Application. Upon successful completion ------------------------------------------ of the Project, SMS agrees to manufacture and offer for sale the Imaging System(s) developed under -11- <PAGE> the Project. The Participants agree that GM shall be a preferred customer for Imaging Systems manufactured by SMS following completion of the Project, with the consequence that such preferred customer shall be entitled, upon written notice SMS received not later than the Project completion or termination, to purchase from SMS up to one-quarter [1/4] of the first twelve [12] Imaging Systems manufactured by SMS at a price which is the lowest of (a) SMS' direct cost and reasonable overhead (according to generally accepted accounting practices, consistently applied) plus a ten percent [10%] fee, or (b) the most favorable price on which SMS offers a substantially similar Imaging System for sale to any person (other than GE) for non-automotive applications, or (c) three percent [3%] less than the most favorable price on which SMS offers a substantially similar Imaging System for sale to any person (other than GE) for automotive applications. In consideration thereof, GM agrees to grant, and hereby grants, to SMS a paid up, irrevocable, worldwide, nonexclusive license under GM's Project Intellectual Property Rights for the practical application of all Subject Invention and/or Project Works owned or controlled by GM in the field of Imaging Systems, together with the right to grant to the purchasers of such Imaging Systems a sublicense to use the same with the purchased Imaging Systems.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.9 Preferred Customers: Practical Application. Upon successful completion ------------------------------------------ of the Project, SMS agrees to use commercially reasonable efforts to manufacture and offer for sale the Imaging System(s) developed under -11- <PAGE> the Project. However, SMS reserves the right to prioritize other business ventures, and there is no guarantee that any Imaging Systems will be manufactured. Notwithstanding the foregoing, GM shall have the option, but not the obligation, to serve as the exclusive manufacturer of Imaging Systems, subject to a separate licensing agreement with SMS.
perturbation.explanation: The original text commits SMS to manufacture and offer for sale the Imaging System and grants GM preferred customer status. The altered version introduces ambiguity by changing 'agrees to manufacture and offer for sale' to 'agrees to use commercially reasonable efforts' and explicitly states 'there is no guarantee.' This creates a direct conflict because the original wording suggests a firm commitment, while the altered version introduces uncertainty. It also directly contradicts the guarantee of preferred customer status by stating GM has the 'option' to manufacture, thus potentially excluding SMS.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.9 Preferred Customers: Practical Application. Upon successful completion ------------------------------------------ of the Project, SMS agrees to use commercially reasonable efforts to manufacture and offer for sale the Imaging System(s) developed under -11- <PAGE> the Project. However, SMS reserves the right to prioritize other business ventures, and there is no guarantee that any Imaging Systems will be manufactured. Notwithstanding the foregoing, GM shall have the option, but not the obligation, to serve as the exclusive manufacturer of Imaging Systems, subject to a separate licensing agreement with SMS.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces uncertainty and optionality ('commercially reasonable efforts,' 'no guarantee,' 'option, but not the obligation') regarding the manufacture and sale of Imaging Systems, which directly contradicts the original commitment implied in the term 'agrees to manufacture and offer for sale,' and the corresponding expectation of GM's preferred customer status. The contradicted text, which is actually the changed text itself, is in contradiction with the earlier text granting GM 'preferred customer' status, because it replaces SMS's obligation to manufacture with a disclaimer and gives GM the option to manufacture instead. This introduces a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 12.6 Amendments. No amendment or modification of this Agreement shall be ---------- valid unless made in writing and signed by all Participants.
perturbation.changed_text: 12.6 Amendments. No amendment or modification of this Agreement shall be ---------- valid unless made in writing and approved by a two-thirds majority of the Management Committee. Any amendment approved in this manner shall be binding on all Participants, even those not explicitly consenting to the amendment.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original requirement for unanimous agreement on amendments. The original text mandates that all Participants must sign an amendment for it to be valid, whereas the edited text allows a two-thirds majority of the Management Committee to approve amendments that become binding on everyone, including dissenting Participants. This contradiction creates uncertainty about the process for altering the agreement and undermines the principle of unanimous consent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12.6 Amendments. No amendment or modification of this Agreement shall be ---------- valid unless made in writing and approved by a two-thirds majority of the Management Committee. Any amendment approved in this manner shall be binding on all Participants, even those not explicitly consenting to the amendment.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 12.6 alters the amendment process from requiring unanimous agreement of all participants to allowing a two-thirds majority of the Management Committee to approve amendments, which are then binding on all participants, even those not consenting. The contradicted text contains the identical verbiage as the changed text, as it should since the contradiction is contained within this section of the agreement. This directly conflicts with the original text's mandate for unanimous consent, creating a strong contradiction regarding the procedure for modifying the agreement.
